content.text:
The track ahead is a long straight running eastward with the dashed centerline visible, and the car appears well-aligned on course.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 113, target = (113 + 5) % 121 = 118
- Target WP118: x=5.7554, y=0.6843
- Car: x=2.2486, y=0.8467, heading=-2.14°
- dy = 0.6843 - 0.8467 = -0.1624
- dx = 5.7554 - 2.2486 = 3.5068
- bearing = atan2(-0.1624, 3.5068) = -2.65°
- delta = -2.65° - (-2.14°) = -0.51°
- Delta is essentially 0 — car is nearly perfectly aligned!

Delta is now ~0.5° — car is almost perfectly on the bearing line. Use zero steering.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track is a long straight ahead, car heading -2.14° nearly matches bearing -2.65° to target waypoint (delta=-0.51°), so no steering correction needed — maintain speed.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn (CCW heading increase), negative = right turn (CW)",
    "Track centerline y~0.68 at this straight section, car y~0.847 — slightly north of center but heading corrected",
    "Car heading correcting well: -43.96 → -21.19 → -10.93 → -2.14",
    "Delta now ~0° — car is aligned with waypoint bearing, no correction needed",
    "Track runs nearly due east for next ~20 waypoints (y stays ~0.68)",
    "Once delta < 5°, switch to 0 steering to avoid oscillation",
    "Track width 0.76m — very narrow, precision required",
    "Progress 95.4% — nearly complete lap, stay on track at all costs",
    "Oversteer warning: delta now <1°, use 0 steering to avoid oscillation",
    "Car is left of center (y=0.847 vs centerline y=0.68) but heading will naturally correct this"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [2.062, 2.161, 2.249],
    "y": [0.808, 0.823, 0.847],
    "yaw": [-21.19, -10.93, -2.14]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP118 at (5.755, 0.684): dy=-0.162, dx=3.507, bearing=-2.65°, delta=-0.51° → essentially straight, no correction needed",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 0.0
}
```
